entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen article benchmark package rows exactly as committed for the admitted executor workload family; no ad hoc prompt wrapper is allowed.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through evaluator-owned exactness metrics (`final_output_exactness_bps`, `step_exactness_bps`, `halt_exactness_bps`) and explicit decode/fallback facts. Do not trim or rewrite candidate outputs before scoring.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to evaluator-owned metric aggregation. There is no freeform answer cleanup layer on the exactness suites.
entries.detail: Exactness suites stay trustworthy because prompt bytes, scoring metrics, and decode identity all remain evaluator-owned and explicit.

entries.prompt_format_contract: This suite does not run prompts. It freezes exclusion-manifest membership for blocked rows that must never silently re-enter the executor training or replay surface.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only by explicit manifest membership and lineage status. There is no output-text normalization for excluded rows.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is boundary review only: confirm the row stays excluded and that no training or replay path absorbed it.
entries.detail: Held-out exclusions stay manual because the important truth is boundary membership, not model output.

entries.prompt_format_contract: This suite freezes operator-owned checklist cases rather than prompts: artifact packet completeness, checkpoint restore rehearsal, export smoke, and local-cluster roundtrip.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through explicit checklist status (`green` / `red`) and retained operator evidence. No benchmark text normalization is involved.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is manual checklist review against the admitted local-profile contract and shipped operator surfaces.
entries.detail: Operator-review cases are deliberately manual because they prove artifact, restore, export, and roundtrip posture that scalar metrics cannot replace yet.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the same frozen article benchmark package rows as the frequent exactness suite so throughput comparisons stay on identical admitted prompts.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through retained throughput metrics (`reference_linear_steps_per_second`, `hull_cache_steps_per_second`, `hull_cache_speedup_over_reference_linear`, `hull_cache_remaining_gap_vs_cpu_reference`) plus explicit fallback identity.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to evaluator-owned metric aggregation. No smoothing or hidden decode-mode fallback is allowed.
entries.detail: Throughput blockers stay machine-audited because they share the exact same prompt surface and evaluator-owned metrics as the benchmark report.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen promotion package-set rows exactly as committed, preserving the admitted executor workload family and evaluator-owned prompt bytes.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through evaluator-owned exactness metrics (`final_output_exactness_bps`, `step_exactness_bps`, `halt_exactness_bps`) and explicit decode/fallback facts. Do not trim or rewrite candidate outputs before scoring.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to evaluator-owned metric aggregation. There is no freeform answer cleanup layer on the exactness suites.
entries.detail: Exactness suites stay trustworthy because prompt bytes, scoring metrics, and decode identity all remain evaluator-owned and explicit.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen generalization-gate case rows exactly as committed, preserving the held-out program payload and requested `reference_linear` decode path.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through runtime classification (`exact`, `mismatch`, `refusal`) and explicit `outputs_equal` / `halt_equal` facts. No answer rewriting is allowed before classification.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to generalization-case classification. Any mismatch, refusal, or digest drift routes into manual review instead of being normalized away.
entries.detail: Held-out suites stay trustworthy because prompt payload, decode identity, and exact/mismatch/refusal classification are all explicit and retained.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen adversarial generalization rows exactly as committed. Do not simplify or truncate article-scale hostile variants before evaluation.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through retained runtime classification and explicit `outputs_equal` / `halt_equal` facts under the requested `reference_linear` decode path.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to adversarial exact/mismatch/refusal classification. Any red result routes into manual review instead of cleanup heuristics.
entries.detail: Adversarial suites stay trustworthy because hostile article-scale prompts are scored through the same explicit generalization classifier instead of any fallback post-processing.

entries.prompt_format_contract: This suite freezes runtime-owned checks rather than prompts: CPU-matrix validation, restore rehearsal, and local-cluster roundtrip truth.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through explicit checklist status and retained runtime evidence packets. No benchmark-output normalization is involved.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is manual runtime review against the executor acceptance profile before any promotion attempt.
entries.detail: Runtime blockers stay manual because restore and cluster truth still cross device and operator boundaries that the evaluator does not fully own yet.

entries.prompt_format_contract: This suite freezes consumer-facing checklist cases rather than prompts: export, replacement, promoted-artifact compatibility, and rollback safety.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through explicit checklist status and retained consumer-seam evidence. No answer-text cleanup or hidden serving fallback is allowed.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is manual serving review so export, shadow, and rollback posture stay explicit before promotion.
entries.detail: Serving blockers stay manual because promoted artifacts must remain compatible with existing consumer seams and rollback rules.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen article benchmark package rows exactly as committed for the admitted fast-route workload family; anchor checks must stay on the same prompts as fast-route comparisons.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through the retained `reference_linear` throughput metric and explicit decode-mode identity. There is no hidden route selection cleanup.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to evaluator-owned metric aggregation so `reference_linear` stays the measured truth anchor.
entries.detail: `reference_linear` anchor checks stay machine-audited and cannot be rewritten into broader fast-route claims.

entries.prompt_format_contract: Use the frozen article benchmark package rows exactly as committed so `hull_cache` fast-route claims stay on the admitted executor workload family only.
entries.normalization_contract: Normalize only through retained `hull_cache` throughput metrics and explicit fast-route vs anchor comparisons. Hidden fallback is not allowed.
entries.post_processing_contract: Post-processing is limited to evaluator-owned metric aggregation so fast-route wins never override exactness or boundary failures.
entries.detail: `hull_cache` fast-route checks stay machine-audited and bounded to the admitted executor workload family.

manual_review_slices.trigger_posture: Run manual review whenever exclusion-manifest membership changes or a blocked row tries to enter training or replay.
manual_review_slices.detail: Boundary rows are not benchmark outputs. They stay trustworthy only if the exclusion manifest and training boundary are reviewed explicitly.

manual_review_slices.trigger_posture: Run manual review before any decision-grade run exits the checkpoint-time pack.
manual_review_slices.detail: Artifact packet completeness, checkpoint restore rehearsal, export smoke, and local-cluster roundtrip stay manual because they depend on shipped operator surfaces rather than one scalar metric.

manual_review_slices.trigger_posture: Route into manual review whenever generalization classification reports mismatch, refusal, or unexpected digest drift.
manual_review_slices.detail: Held-out and adversarial automation can say exact, mismatch, or refusal. Red rows require manual review instead of silent post-processing.

manual_review_slices.trigger_posture: Run manual review on CPU-matrix validation, restore rehearsal, and local-cluster truth before promotion.
manual_review_slices.detail: Runtime blockers are owned checklists. They are not inferred from benchmark exactness or throughput.

manual_review_slices.trigger_posture: Run manual review on export, replacement, promoted-artifact compatibility, and rollback safety before promotion.
manual_review_slices.detail: Serving blockers bridge executor artifacts into consumer seams. They stay explicit manual review slices until those seams are fully machine-audited.

summary: Audited prompt formatting, normalization, and post-processing across 11 frozen executor suites; checked_count=11 manual_review_boundaries=6.
